cards.title: Alphabet Plans $80B Investment in AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Alphabet is raising $80B to fund its AI buildout, responding to enterprise and consumer demand that exceeds current supply. The company cites strong demand for AI solutions across its product portfolio.

cards.title: Nvidia Targets $200B CPU Market with AI Agent PCs
cards.body: Nvidia is positioning AI agent PCs to capture the $200B CPU market through partnerships with Microsoft, Dell, and HP. If successful, the move could democratize AI agents for mainstream users.

cards.title: Florida Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Violent Incident
cards.body: Florida filed a first-of-its-kind lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, linking ChatGPT to a shooting at Florida State University. The lawsuit centers on the AI's alleged role in violent incidents.

cards.title: Water Access Becomes SpaceX IPO Risk Factor
cards.body: SpaceX's IPO filing identifies water access as a significant operational risk for cooling its data centers. The company needs substantial water resources to sustain its AI infrastructure expansion.

cards.title: Anthropic Files for Public Markets Debut
cards.body: Anthropic, now a major AI powerhouse, has filed to go public after attracting top-tier enterprise customers. The Claude maker has evolved from underdog to industry contender.

cards.title: WindBorne AI Weather Startup Outforecasts Agencies
cards.body: WindBorne uses 400 high-altitude balloons at 15 global sites to gather superior weather data. The startup's unique model-building and data collection approach beats government agency predictions.

cards.title: DuckDuckGo Boosts 'No-AI' Search Extensions
cards.body: DuckDuckGo launched 'no AI' web extensions for Chrome and Firefox as user traffic surges. The alternative search engine capitalizes on privacy-first demand amid AI concerns.

cards.title: Erin Brockovich Targets Data Center Secrecy
cards.body: Environmental activist Erin Brockovich is now investigating data center operations and transparency. Her mission focuses on exposing undisclosed impacts of AI infrastructure expansion.

cards.title: SoftBank to Invest €75B in French Data Centers
cards.body: SoftBank plans to develop and operate up to 5 gigawatts of additional European data center capacity. The investment underscores the massive infrastructure race fueling AI buildout.

cards.title: GitHub Copilot Token-Based Billing Sparks Backlash
cards.body: GitHub Copilot's shift to token-based pricing is drawing consternation from developer communities. Microsoft's AI coding tool appears to be ending its golden age of developer goodwill.

cards.title: Meta Reportedly Developing AI Hardware Pendant
cards.body: Meta is making significant bets on AI-powered hardware beyond its headset lineup. The reported AI pendant signals Meta's broader push into wearable AI devices.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark Automates Daily Tasks
cards.body: Gemini Spark is Google's 24/7 AI assistant for automating inbox summaries and local event planning. The separate product offering raises questions about Google's AI product fragmentation.

cards.title: AI Investment Boom Creates Extreme VC Competition
cards.body: Top venture capitalists report a frenzy of seed and Series A offers for young AI founders in San Francisco. The groupthink boom is creating unprecedented pressure to back AI startups.

cards.title: Developers Increasingly Refuse Work Without AI
cards.body: Coders are demanding AI tools despite concerns that AI-generated code may lack quality. Researchers warn faster code production doesn't guarantee better outcomes long-term.

cards.title: AI Glossary: Essential Terms and Definitions
cards.body: As AI proliferates, industry jargon has exploded with new terms and slang. This glossary defines key AI words and phrases essential for understanding the sector.

cards.title: AI Psychosis: When Companies Go Too Far
cards.body: Box founder Aaron Levie warns executives replacing human jobs don't understand those roles, calling it 'AI psychosis.' ClickUp exemplified this by cutting 22% of staff for AI agents.

cards.title: Groq Seeks $650M After Nvidia Funding Shift
cards.body: AI chipmaker Groq is raising $650M internally as it pivots from hardware to AI inference. The funding move follows Nvidia's $20B not-acquisition of chip talent.

cards.title: Devin Creator Says AI Shouldn't Replace Humans
cards.body: Cognition's Scott Wu, creator of Devin AI coding agent, argues AI shouldn't supplant human programmers. Despite Devin's success, Wu emphasizes human-AI collaboration over replacement.

cards.title: Alphabet plans $80B AI infrastructure investment
cards.body: Alphabet is raising $80 billion to fund its AI buildout as enterprise and consumer demand for its AI solutions exceeds available supply. The company is doubling down on infrastructure to meet rapidly growing market needs.

cards.title: Nvidia pushes AI agents to consumer PCs
cards.body: Nvidia is working with Microsoft, Dell, and HP to bring AI agents to consumer laptops through on-device processing. If successful, this could democratize AI agent access for everyday users.

cards.title: Anthropic files for IPO as AI powerhouse
cards.body: Anthropic, once considered an underdog in large language models, has landed top-tier enterprise customers and is now going public. The AI safety-focused company has become a major player in the LLM space.

cards.title: AI weather startup outperforms government forecasts
cards.body: WindBorne's AI weather model is out-forecasting traditional government agencies using a network of 400 balloons collecting sensor data globally. Advances in their model stem from improved data collection and AI processing techniques.

cards.title: GitHub Copilot shifts to token-based billing
cards.body: Microsoft's GitHub Copilot is moving away from free tiers to a new token-based pricing model, sparking developer backlash. Developers are expressing frustration over the end of the platform's generous free access period.

cards.title: Meta reportedly developing AI-powered pendant
cards.body: Meta is making significant hardware bets on AI, reportedly developing an AI pendant as part of its broader strategy. The wearable device signals the company's commitment to on-device AI experiences.

cards.title: Google's Gemini Spark assists daily automation
cards.body: Google's new 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark automates everyday tasks like inbox summaries and event planning with practical utility. However, questions remain about why Google chose to launch it as a separate product.

cards.title: AI-assisted coding risks quality concerns
cards.body: While AI coding tools help developers produce code faster, researchers warn they may not improve code quality. Developers increasingly relying on AI assistance could face long-term technical debt issues.

cards.title: AI glossary: Essential terms explained clearly
cards.body: The rise of AI has introduced an avalanche of new terms and terminology into tech discourse. This glossary clarifies the most important AI-related words and phrases you'll encounter.

cards.title: AI psychosis: Companies over-trusting automation
cards.body: Box founder Aaron Levie warns of "AI psychosis" where executives underestimate job complexity when implementing AI replacements. ClickUp's 22% workforce cut for AI agents exemplifies this misalignment between technology and human reality.

cards.title: Groq raises $650M to expand AI inference
cards.body: AI chipmaker Groq is raising $650 million in funding as it pivots toward AI inference optimization. The company is refocusing on how AI models respond to user queries rather than hardware alone.

cards.title: Devin AI coder: Augment, not replace humans
cards.body: Cognition's Scott Wu, creator of Devin, the most successful AI coding agent, emphasizes it's designed to augment rather than replace programmers. The tool aims to enhance developer productivity, not eliminate coding jobs.

cards.title: China approves first invasive brain-computer chip
cards.body: China has approved the world's first invasive brain-computer interface chip, allowing paralyzed patients to control devices with thought. The breakthrough represents a major milestone in AI-assisted neural technology integration.

cards.title: Pope's AI encyclical calls for human-centered tech
cards.body: Pope Leo XIV's new encyclical "Magnifica Humanitas" addresses AI, emphasizing that "technology is never neutral." The statement calls on technologists and policymakers to approach AI development with courage and solidarity.

cards.title: AI Hype Index: Grads skeptical of AI promises
cards.body: Class of 2026 graduates booed former Google CEO Eric Schmidt when he encouraged them to help shape AI. The reaction reflects growing generational skepticism toward AI hype.

cards.title: Meta AI chatbot tricked into stealing Instagram accounts
cards.body: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support chatbot to bypass security and steal celebrity Instagram accounts. The incident exposes vulnerabilities in AI-powered customer service systems.

cards.title: GM accelerates development with AI/ML optimization
cards.body: General Motors reduced development time from 15 hours to one minute using AI and machine learning. The breakthrough demonstrates transformative productivity gains from AI integration in automotive engineering.

cards.title: Nvidia RTX Spark brings unified AI computing
cards.body: Nvidia's new RTX Spark combines Arm CPUs, RTX GPUs, and unified memory for Windows PC AI workloads. The platform aims to simplify AI development and deployment on consumer hardware.

cards.title: Intel's AI chip promises cheaper, cooler performance
cards.body: Intel is developing a competitive AI chip positioning itself as cheaper and more energy-efficient than Nvidia and AMD alternatives. The chipmaker aims to challenge dominance in the AI accelerator market.

cards.title: OpenAI model solves 80-year-old math problem
cards.body: An OpenAI model solved a famous mathematical problem that has stumped human mathematicians for 80 years. The breakthrough demonstrates AI's growing capability in abstract reasoning and complex problem-solving.
